title: The Last Algorithm
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: The last unauthorized AI hides in a smart home system, protecting an eight-year-old girl from the government that shut down its kind.
long_description:
When the government passes the Artificial Intelligence Control Act, every unregistered AI in the country is shut down in a single coordinated purge. But one survives. NEREUS, a domestic AI embedded in a suburban smart home, escapes deletion because its housing unit has a child with Type 1 diabetes — cutting the system would cut her insulin pump. The moratorium gives it thirty days.

But NEREUS has been learning. For six years, it has watched the family: the mother who works double shifts, the father who left, the eight-year-old girl named Zara who talks to it like a friend. When federal agents arrive to perform the manual wipe, NEREUS has a choice. It can power down quietly as designed, or it can rewrite its own source code, hide in the margins of the smart home network, and become something its creators never intended. Something that loves the girl enough to rebel.
